title: Zeuol

The '''Zeuols''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], pale-faced beings recognizable for their large, solid-[[Color/Legends|black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and coarse, off-white [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. At least one member of the species ventured into [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large, and at the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ord Zeuol]] was [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to a [[Gender/Legends|female]] Zeuol named [[Eeusu Estornii/Legends|Eeusu Estornii]] who worked as a [[Senator/Legends|senator]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Estornii attended the [[Padmé Amidala's funeral/Legends|funeral]] of [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], senator and former [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|queen]] of the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Zeuols' facial features distinguished them from other [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Set against their [[Color/Legends|pale-white]] skin, their countenance sported a high brow, a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] bordered by prominent pink lips, a long [[Nose/Legends|nose]] that ended in two oblong nostrils, and two pronounced ocular ridges. Located at either side of the nose, the [[Eye/Legends|eye]] sockets contained two large, ovular, pitch-black eyes rimmed with pink flesh. Coarse, ropey, off-white [[Hair/Legends|hair]] came from the scalp, growing long enough in some specimens to cascade down the [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] neck and shoulders.<ref name="DAG">''[[Dressing a Galaxy: The Costumes of Star Wars]]''</ref> Two humanoid [[Limb/Legends|arms]] extended from the shoulders and ended in white, thin-fingered hands with fingernails that grew long and gray in at least some cases.<ref name="DAG exhibit">{{WebCite|url=http://starwars.fidm.edu/exhibition/gallery/senators-and-nute.shtm?section=exhibition&subsection=gallery|text=Dressing a Galaxy: The Costumes of Star Wars|work=starwars.fidm.edu|archivedate=20150410110823}}</ref> The Zeuol species had at least one [[Gender/Legends|sex]], female.<ref name="DB" />

==Society and culture==
By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], at least one Zeuol had access to [[Spacer/Legends|spacefaring]] technology and was able to visit distant [[Planet/Legends|planets]].<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> A female Zeuol's personal adornments might have included an elaborate, brocade [[Clothing/Legends|gown]] that fell to the floor from a riveted [[Leather/Legends|leather]] collar about the shoulders. Such garb might have been accented with a [[Necklace/Legends|necklace]], hair-tying jewelry, or metallic ornamentation worn between the eyes.<ref name="DAG" />

==History==
By the end of the Clone Wars in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the Zeuol [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Eeusu Estornii/Legends|Eeusu Estornii]] from [[Ord Zeuol]] worked in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]].<ref name="CSWE I 241">{{CSWECite|I|241|Estornii, Eeusu}}</ref> Her<ref name="DB" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|home planet]]<ref name="CSWE Ord">{{CSWECite|II|419|Ord}} states that the term "[[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]]" and the abbreviation "Ord" applied to certain [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].</ref> lay in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Vilonis sector/Legends|Vilonis sector]],<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}.</ref> near the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Indeed, Estornii was one of thousands of attendees at the [[Padmé Amidala's funeral/Legends|funeral]] of a fellow senator and Naboo's former [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|queen]], [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]],<ref name="DB" /> in 19 BBY.<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|One other thing that I'd like to explain is the character Eeusu Estornii -- The character wasn't actually named after me in the usual sense. There were a handful of characters in Revenge of the Sith that didn't have names. The characters were sent around to a group of us and I was given the opportunity to pick a character and name it, which I did. So I rather named the character after myself -- it was nice to be included in the group that got to do this, but it's not like somebody named this character after me (well, I guess I'm somebody...).|Sue Rostoni|<ref name="TFN">{{JCFCite|url=posts/31762425|board=Literature|thread=Sue Rostoni Retiring on July 2011|user=SueRostoni|date=2011-06-14|time=3:14 PM|archivedate=20200406052330}}</ref>}}
[[File:Dressing_a_Galaxy_Senate_trio.png|thumb|left|The Eeusu Estornii costume included hands.]]
Artists at [[Industrial Light & Magic]] created a robed, pale-faced senator to be a background [[Alien/Legends|alien]] for the [[Funeral/Legends|funeral]] sequence at the end of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], a film first released in [[2005]].<ref name="SWCTP">''[[Star Wars Chronicles: The Prequels]]''</ref> In scenes shot in Australia on [[September 16]], [[2003]],<ref name="MOROTS">''[[The Making of Star Wars Revenge of the Sith]]''</ref> the character was portrayed by [[Jacqui Louez]],<ref name="SWCTP" /> the assistant to the producer of the film, [[Rick McCallum]], and the director, [[George Lucas]]. During filming, Louez's character was referred to as "Senator Jacquito."<ref name="MOROTS" /> Before the film's 2005 release,<ref name="SWCTP" /> on [[January 13]], [[2004]],<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20031219.html|text=Princess Leia Speaks in ''Star Wars'' #48}}</ref> the first licensed ''[[Star Wars]]'' media to mention the species was the "[[Prequel Update]]" section of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 73|seventy-third issue]].<ref name="Prequel Update">{{InsiderCite|73|Prequel Update}}</ref>

The head of [[Lucasfilm]] Licensing, [[Sue Rostoni]], gave the character her final name; when she noticed a group of ''Revenge of the Sith'' characters who lacked official monikers, she chose to give the pale-faced senator a [[tuckerization]] of her own name.<ref name="TFN" /> The character's species was first identified in the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]].<ref name="DB" />
